LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DARION ALBERT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION - THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS WITH 60-DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF MR. ALBERT CAN STATE A CLAIM AGAINST THE TRANSPORATION AGENCY THAT THEY BREACHED AN OBLIGATION TO HIM SOUNDING NEGLIGENCE/THAT THEY HAD A DUTY TO DO THINGS THAT THEY FAILED TO DO. THE COURT WILL PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Aug-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Darion Albert (415-879-5465) appears as plaintiff/moving party, In Propria Persona

Deputy City Attorney, James M. Emery (415-554-4628) specially appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency

Court hears from plaintiff and explains tentative ruling and proceedings as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF DARION ALBERT'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings denied without prejudice to being refiled in the event that he files an amended complaint. Mr. Albert's motion is based on the apparent misunderstanding that he can move for judgment on the pleadings without having availed himself of the opportunity to file an amended complaint, as was provided to him in the June 6, 2016 order. On its own motion, the court gives Mr. Albert another 30 days to file an amended complaint alleging that the San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency breached an obligation to him sounding in negligence. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)

Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Darion Albert telephone number 415-879-5465 for

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court changed its ruling. In re: PLAINTIFF DARION ALBERT'S Motion For Reconsideration - The Court grants the motion for judgment on the pleadings with 60-days leave to amend if Mr. Albert can state a claim against the Transporation Agency that they breached an obligation to him sounding negligence/that they had a duty to do things that they failed to do. The Court will prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Hearing Of Defendant Sfmta Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff DARION ALBERT, Tel No. 415-879-5465 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court finds Plaintiff did not notify the opposing side of his intent to appear today to contest the Court's tentive ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges that defendant failed to comply with the notice requirements of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ("FCRA") (15 U.S.C. sec. 1681 et seq.) FCRA does not apply to this case. The allegations of the complaint indicate that defendant was not acting as a prospective employer for plaintiff. Defendant was merely processing a permit application. The purpose of the FCRA is to ensure accurate credit reporting. This case is unrelated to credit reporting. A criminal background report and a consumer credit report are two different reports. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. The Court invites Plaintiff to file a motion for reconsideration of the Court's order if Plaintiff believes he has grounds to do so. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
